blocks.content: He left at the corner. I went inside.
My mom was at the kitchen table with her coffee and the Saturday paper. She looked up when I came in — not the regular look. The one she'd been giving me since Thursday when I told her about Daniel.
She nodded. Looked back at her paper. Then:

blocks.speaker: Mom
blocks.content: I'm going to call the school Monday. I've been deciding and I'm done deciding.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know. I think it's the right call.
She looked at me like I'd surprised her.

blocks.speaker: Mom
blocks.content: When did you start thinking that?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: When Mr. Cole said he wasn't always going to pull up at the right time.
She was quiet for a moment. Then she reached over and put her hand on mine — just for a second.

blocks.speaker: Mom
blocks.content: I'm proud of how you handled it. Not everyone would have.
